title: Encyclia trachychila
taxonomy: Encyclia trachychila belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a species of orchid. Within the Orchidaceae family, it is classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae. This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, and Encyclia trachychila is part of the Cranichideae tribe. The genus Encyclia itself is a group of epiphytic orchids native to the Americas. Further classification within the species is not well-documented.

intro: Encyclia trachychila, also known as the "Yellow Encyclia," is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Encyclia trachychila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Encyclia trachychila is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, as well as to reduce fever and promote relaxation.
